commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Android onboarding recovery mapping from structured gateway auth failure codes and recommended next steps to more specific user-facing recovery actions, with focused unit coverage.

PR surface: Other +84. Total +84 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main preserves structured gateway auth details but Android recovery copy collapses several auth failures into generic messaging. I did not run a live Android setup session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor revision plus real Android proof; because proof is missing, this should stay human-review/author-action rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: fix(android): clarify gateway auth recovery states
target.body:
Closes #98046

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Android users recovering gateway setup would see generic gateway-unreachable/authentication messaging when the gateway had already returned a structured auth failure such as an expired setup code, stale saved credential, missing token, or password mismatch.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Android recovery detail now consumes the existing `GatewayConnectionProblem` code and recommended next-step fields before falling back to generic copy. Pairing approval handling stays on the existing approval-command path, and this change does not add new gateway protocol fields.

## User Impact

Android setup recovery now gives users a specific next action: scan a fresh QR when a bootstrap setup code expired, re-authenticate or reset stale saved credentials, re-enter missing or invalid gateway auth, or review gateway auth configuration when the gateway reports that path.

## Evidence

- Claim proved: the Android recovery mapper now distinguishes existing structured gateway auth detail codes instead of falling through to `Gateway unreachable`.
- Commands / artifacts:
  - `git diff --check` passed.
  - `python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local` passed with `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
  - Added focused unit coverage in `OnboardingFlowLogicTest` for bootstrap expiry, stale credential/device-token mismatch, password mismatch, missing token, device identity required, recommended-next-step fallback, and unknown-message fallback.
- Observed result: the local diff is limited to `apps/android/app/src/main/java/ai/openclaw/app/ui/OnboardingFlow.kt` and `apps/android/app/src/test/java/ai/openclaw/app/ui/OnboardingFlowLogicTest.kt`.
- Not tested / proof gaps: local Android Gradle execution did not reach tests because the wrapper could not download `https://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-9.5.1-bin.zip`; `gradlew.bat --version` failed with `SocketTimeoutException: Read timed out` at the wrapper's configured `networkTimeout=10000`. No live Android device/emulator setup session was available in this run.
target.author: qingminglong
